term_json: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition"},{"type":"text","text":": The voice or character through which the story is told."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"unreliable narrator"},{"type":"text","text":" is one whose POV is not trustworthy (e.g. due to lying, mental health issues)"}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is the central idea, message, or underlying meaning in a book."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A key characteristic of a theme is its universality, which transcends the characters or events in the book. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\"Lord of the Flies\": behavior of children is unacceptable in all cultures."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\"Grapes of Wrath\": the inhumanity of capitalism and the vitality of family and friendship"}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is the speed at which a story is told. It determines how quickly (or slowly) the reader experiences the story events."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pacing helps to control emotional tension, information flow and balance fast-paced action with slower, more reflective scenes."}]}]}

Pre-publication copy used for reviews."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Backlist:"},{"type":"text","text":" Older books published by an author, not their newest release."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Book Hangover:"},{"type":"text","text":" The inability to start a new book because you are still emotionally invested in the last one."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Book Haul:"},{"type":"text","text":" A large number of books bought at once."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"CR:"},{"type":"text","text":" Currently Reading."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"DNF:"},{"type":"text","text":" Did Not Finish. When a reader abandons a book."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Reading Slump:"},{"type":"text","text":" A period where a reader cannot seem to get into any book."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"TBR:"},{"type":"text","text":" \"To Be Read\" pile or list "}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

A book is generally said to have “passed” if the book has at least two female characters who talk to each other about something other than men."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Unreliable Narrator:"},{"type":"text","text":" A narrator whose POV is not trustworthy (could be due to lying, misinformation on the part of the MC, mental health or memory issues, etc.)."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Morally Gray:"},{"type":"text","text":" A character who straddles the line between being likable and unlikable, or villainous and heroic. This person often has more bad qualities than good, but the reader can see the positives peeking through."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Cinnamon Roll:"},{"type":"text","text":" A really sweet and lovable character."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Grumpy/Sunshine:"},{"type":"text","text":" A pairing of a generally grumpy character with a sweet, happy character."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is a story within a story. It is another story, hidden under the surface story."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In most allegories, the hidden story has something to do with politics, religion, or morality — complex subjects that are difficult to understand directly. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples"},{"type":"text","text":" (examples taken "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/allegory/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":null}},{"type":"underline"}],"text":"from this source"},{"type":"text","text":")"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"George Orwell’s "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Animal Farm"},{"type":"text","text":" is one of literature’s most famous allegories. The surface story is about a group of farm animals who rise up, kick out the humans, and try to run the farm themselves. The hidden story, however, is about the Russian Revolution, and each of the "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/character/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Character"}}],"text":"characters"},{"type":"text","text":" represents some figure from that revolution."}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Seuss wrote "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"The Sneetches"},{"type":"text","text":" as an allegory for racism and other forms of prejudice. The story is all about creatures who are treated as inferior because they don’t have stars on their bellies. Like all Dr. Seuss stories, it’s written in a child-friendly, playful style, but it still contains an important political message."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The antagonist in a story is characterized many times as a villain but it could simply be any character who gets in the way of the protagonist (main character). "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Every story must have at least one protagonist but antagonists are optional."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A short story or "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/fable/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Fable"}}],"text":"fable "},{"type":"text","text":"which provides a simple moral lesson. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Apologues are often told through the use of animal "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/character/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Character"}}],"text":"characters"},{"type":"text","text":" with symbolical elements."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"autobiography"},{"type":"text","text":" is a self-written life story whereas a "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"biography"},{"type":"text","text":" is a life story written by someone else."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The very end of a story, where all the different plot lines are finally tied up and all remaining questions answered. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It happens right after the "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"climax"},{"type":"text","text":" of the story, and it shows the aftermath of that climax, giving the reader some hints as to what will happen next. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The denouement is usually the very last thing your audience sees, so it has to be well-written or the "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/story/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Story"}}],"text":"story "},{"type":"text","text":"will seem unsatisfying."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"An optional final chapter of a story, such as in a play or book, and which may serve a variety of purposes—concluding or bringing closure to events, wrapping up loose ends, reporting the eventual fates of "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/character/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Character"}}],"text":"characters"},{"type":"text","text":" after the main story, commenting on the events that have unfolded, and or "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/setting/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Setting"}}],"text":"setting "},{"type":"text","text":"up a sequel."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fiction that concentrates on imaginary elements (the fantastic). "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This can mean magic, the supernatural, alternate worlds, superheroes, monsters, fairies, magical creatures, mythological heroes—essentially, anything that an author can imagine outside of reality."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It’s a "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/genre/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Genre"}}],"text":"genre "},{"type":"text","text":"of literature where the author writes about his or her memories, usually going back to childhood."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"A parable is a short "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/story/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Story"}}],"text":"story "},{"type":"text","text":"used to illustrate a moral or spiritual lesson. It comes from a Greek word meaning “comparison.”"}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples"}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Good Samaritan"}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Boy Who Cried Wolf"}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is “the use of humor, irony, exaggeration, or ridicule to expose and criticize people’s stupidity or vices.”"}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Most satires are humorous, ironic, "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"and"},{"type":"text","text":" exaggerated, but they only have to be "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"one"},{"type":"text","text":" of these things to count as satire."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is a short scene that captures a single moment or a defining detail about a character, idea, or other element of the story. Vignettes are mostly descriptive; they often include little or no "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/plot/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Plot"}}],"text":"plot "},{"type":"text","text":"detail. "}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"They are not stand-alone literary works, nor are they complete plots or "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://literaryterms.net/narrative/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":"Narrative"}}],"text":"narratives"},{"type":"text","text":". They are small parts of a larger work, and can only exist as pieces of a whole story."}]}]}
